result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d make it so that power reliably came with empathy.

Not perfect virtue, not utopia, not everyone agreeing — just a deep human constraint where the more influence someone has over other lives, the more vividly they feel the consequences of their choices.

A CEO deciding layoffs, a general ordering strikes, a politician cutting healthcare, a judge sentencing someone, a billionaire shaping public life — all of them would have to experience, emotionally and concretely, the human reality downstream of their decisions.

I think a lot of the world’s cruelty survives because distance makes it easy. If distance stopped numbing people, many systems would change very quickly.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
